
## Result Summary

The guarded harness configured with -A Win32, built brender_core_float.lib plus all three smoke executables, direct-ran brender_core_render_smoke.exe with exit 0 (320x240 RGB_888, 1051 lit pixels, eight cube vertices projected inside the viewport, PPM written), and passed 3/3 CTest cases with -C Debug.

## Artifacts Policy

The materialized harness, configure tree, build tree, smoke executables, the brender_core_float library, and the generated PPM render output were kept outside this metadata repo. No generated binaries, copied BRender source, private assets, or restricted SDK material are committed.

## Public Notes

This attempt is the first BRender revival milestone that produces an actual rendered image rather than only starting the framework. The generated harness materializes a brender_core_render_smoke target that starts BRender, allocates a BR_PMT_RGB_888 memory pixelmap, builds a real BrMatrix4Perspective projection, transforms a unit cube through BrMatrix4Mul and BrMatrix4ApplyP, draws the twelve edges with BrPixelmapLine, reads pixels back with BrPixelmapPixelGet to self-verify, and dumps a 320x240 PPM. Every plotting call flows through the pure-C memory pixelmap dispatch in core/pixelmap/pmmem.c backed by the harness _Mem* primitives; the assembly softrend driver is not used. The program produced 1051 lit pixels and passed as CTest case 3 of 3 under a Visual Studio Win32 Debug target. It does not claim x64 portability, shaded or textured rendering, the native datafile save path, drivers, warning cleanup, or release packaging.

## Blockers

- the render smoke still requires a 32-bit C target because the unreworked period code stores some pointer-shaped values in 32-bit fields
- rendering coverage is limited to a wireframe drawn through the pure-C memory pixelmap dispatch; the assembly softrend rasterizer, shaded triangles, textures, and z-buffering are not exercised
- the portable core and host fallback stubs still lack semantic tests for the DOS, driver, and rendering behavior they stand in for
- image output is a raw PPM dump of the pixelmap buffer; the native BrPixelmapSave datafile path and drivers are not built yet

## Next Actions

- add semantic tests for the portable core and memory fallback functions before treating the wireframe path as a production renderer
- extend the render smoke toward filled and shaded primitives once the generic renderer path is audited
- translate the softrend driver so the hardware-free rasterizer covers triangles, textures, and depth
- keep generated binaries and PPM output out of git unless an intentional release package is reviewed
